epic.name: On-Call Calendar UI
epic.objective: Build the frontend for viewing and managing on-call schedules, including a calendar view of upcoming shifts and override management.
epic.definition_of_done: Users can view who is currently on call, see upcoming shifts in a calendar, and create overrides — all from the web UI.

tasks.name: On-call schedules list and detail pages
tasks.description: Schedule list page and schedule detail page showing current on-call user and layer configuration
acceptance_criteria: GET /on-call → SchedulesPage: list all schedules with current on-call user badge
GET /on-call/:id → ScheduleDetailPage: name, timezone, layers list, current on-call user prominently displayed
Layer card: shows rotation type, participants in order, current shift highlighted
Sidebar nav item: Calendar icon, label 'On-call', path /on-call
Empty state for no schedules with CTA to create first schedule
Loading skeleton and error states
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow existing page patterns (IncidentsListPage, RoutingRulesPage). Add useSchedules and useSchedule hooks. The 'current on-call' badge calls GET /api/v1/schedules/:id/oncall.

tasks.name: Shift calendar view
tasks.description: Calendar component that renders the on-call timeline for the next 4 weeks
acceptance_criteria: Calendar renders a 2-week or 4-week grid (week rows, day columns)
Each day cell shows the on-call user(s) for that day derived from /oncall/timeline
Color-coded by participant (consistent hash → color)
Navigate forward/back by week
Overrides visually distinct from base rotation (striped or different shade)
Responsive: collapses to list view on mobile
tasks.implementation_notes: Build a simple custom calendar grid rather than importing a heavy library. Use the /oncall/timeline endpoint with ?from=&to= to fetch the rendered schedule. Color assignment: stable hash of user_name → HSL hue.

tasks.name: Override management UI
tasks.description: UI for creating and deleting schedule overrides
acceptance_criteria: Override button on ScheduleDetailPage opens OverrideModal
OverrideModal fields: override_user (text input), start_time (datetime-local), end_time (datetime-local)
Validation: end_time > start_time, user required
Created override immediately reflected in calendar view
Delete override button on each override entry with confirmation
Toast notifications for create/delete
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow the same inline modal pattern as RoutingRulesPage. Override list shown below the calendar on ScheduleDetailPage.

tasks.name: Schedule create/edit UI
tasks.description: Forms for creating schedules and managing rotation layers
acceptance_criteria: Create Schedule modal: name, description, timezone (searchable dropdown of IANA zones)
Add Layer form on ScheduleDetailPage: name, rotation_type, rotation_start, shift_duration, participants (ordered list with add/remove)
Participants can be added as free-text Slack user IDs or display names
Edit schedule metadata (name, description, timezone)
Delete schedule with confirmation (warns if active)
tasks.implementation_notes: Timezone dropdown: use a hardcoded list of ~50 common IANA zones for v0.4 simplicity rather than shipping the full tz database to the frontend.
